commit.message:
Convert from AppDaemon to native HA custom integration (v2.0)

Major architectural change: the controller is now a native Home Assistant
custom integration instead of an AppDaemon app.

What changed:
- Install via HACS as type Integration (no more AppDaemon dependency)
- Config flow UI wizard replaces manual YAML editing
- Number entities with sliders replace input_number helpers
- Sensor entity for active rule replaces input_text helper
- Switch entity for notifications replaces input_boolean helper
- Uses weather.get_forecasts service (future-proof, replaces deprecated attribute)
- All entities grouped under a single device in Settings

What stayed the same:
- The pure decide() function and all 15 test cases (zero logic changes)
- All decision rules, priorities, and safety floor behaviour
- Notification messages and targets
- Dashboard card (updated entity names)

commit.message:
add: AES congested validation — η beats RUDY (R²=0.40 vs 0.11)

AES nangate45 (16591 cells, 146K overflow, demand proxy):
  R²(η, demand) = 0.40 at r=3·diag
  R²(RUDY, demand) = 0.11

η outperforms RUDY on larger congested design (opposite of gcd).
But both R² are low (subsampling 5000/16591 cells) and target is
demand proxy (F2 leakage caveat still applies).

Note: this is demand proxy, not independent GRT ground truth.
Independent validation (Issue #19) showed +2.6% on gcd.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Auto-sync builder cache to GitHub repo after every lookup

The builder cache now automatically pushes itself back to the GitHub
repo after every builder lookup completes and after every manual
contact save. This means:

1. App runs builder lookup → finds phone/email → saves to cache
2. Cache auto-syncs to GitHub via API → builder-cache.json in repo updated
3. Next deploy → pulls the updated cache from repo → all contacts instant
4. No manual steps, no volume needed — the repo IS the storage

Requires GITHUB_TOKEN env var set on Railway.
Token: set in Railway dashboard → Variables → GITHUB_TOKEN

commit.message:
Add O_TMPFILE/O_NOFOLLOW to OpenFlags + apk cache write investigation

Added O_TMPFILE (returns ENOSYS) and O_NOFOLLOW to OpenFlags.
apk update fetches indices from Alpine mirrors but can't save them
("Permission denied"). The error is NOT from our EACCES checks (which
bypass for root). It's from apk's internal error mapping of a different
failure — likely renameat2 with unsupported flags or an ext4 write
edge case. The fetch itself works (18 packages detected).

Contract tests: 118/118 PASS

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Extract phone/email from permit portal APIs — data was there all along

CRITICAL FIX: The EnerGov contacts API returns PhoneNumber and
EmailAddress fields on contact objects, but the code was only
extracting FirstName, LastName, and CompanyName — completely
ignoring the phone and email sitting right there.

Charleston (EnerGov):
- Building permit contacts API: now extracts PhoneNumber,
  BusinessPhone, CellPhone, EmailAddress from contractor/applicant
- Inspection contacts fallback: same extraction
- Also passes builder_phone/email through the enrichment pipeline
  instead of losing them

EnerGov Generic (Berkeley, Hilton Head, Bluffton):
- Added ContractorPhone, PhoneNumber, ContractorEmail, EmailAddress

North Charleston (ArcGIS):
- Added ContractorPhone, Phone, ContractorEmail, Email fields

Mount Pleasant (Oracle OPAL):
- Added contractorPhone, builderPhone, contractorEmail, builderEmail

This means phone/email comes directly from the permit data itself
for many builders — no web scraping needed at all.

commit.message:
add: independent ground truth validation (Issue #19/F2)

Extracted per-GCell guide-based usage from OpenROAD GRT via
grt::write_segments (independent of net bounding boxes used for RUDY).

Against INDEPENDENT ground truth (GRT guide usage):
  cell_density alone: CV-R² = 0.806
  η + density (r=2d): CV-R² = 0.832 (+2.6%)

η genuinely adds information beyond density, confirmed against
ground truth that shares NO data with the RUDY predictor.

The +2.6% is smaller than the +13.8% against demand proxy,
confirming Codex's leakage concern was partially valid. But the
signal is real and cross-validated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: reduce API calls with persistent caching and score reuse

- GeminiEmbeddingProvider: persistent disk cache (.cache/embeddings/)
  saves SHA256-keyed embeddings as JSON, auto-saves every 10 new entries;
  eliminates redundant embedding API calls across runs
- Reranker: reuse candidate.dense_score as precomputed semantic similarity
  avoids re-embedding during Stage 2 precision reranking (~16 calls saved)
- QueryExpansion: disk cache per goal_id (.cache/expansions/{goal_id}.json)
  expansion Gemini call skipped on repeated runs (4.46s → 0.0002s)

fix: remove Button/Commands from form XML to fix loading hang

Each Button element in extension form XML causes 1C loading to hang
during "Завершение загрузки конфигурации":
- Without CommandName (v2.0.8): AutoCommandBar resolution loop → hang at 84%
- With CommandName + <Commands> (v2.0.9): schema parse issue → hang at 47%

Fix: remove all Button/Commands from form XML entirely (revert to v2.0.5
structure that is confirmed to load). Implement save via ПередЗакрытием
handler in BSL — data is validated and saved automatically on form close.

v2.1.0 changes:
- builder.py: form_content() always Autofill=true, no <Commands> block
- builder.py: _form_items_xml() no longer generates Button elements
- bsl_templates.py: form_module_mapping() replaces КнопкаСохранитьНажатие(Команда)
  with ПередЗакрытием(Отказ, ЗавершениеРаботы, ТекстПредупреждения, СтандартнаяОбработка)
  — standard 8.3.6+ managed form close handler with validation + auto-save

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Track builder-cache.json in git — persist contacts across deploys

ROOT CAUSE: builder-cache.json was in .gitignore. Every deploy to
Railway started with an empty cache, so all builder phone/email
data was lost and had to be re-scraped from scratch.

FIX: Removed builder-cache.json from .gitignore so it deploys with
the code. Now the flow is:

1. Builder lookup finds a phone/email → saves to builder-cache.json
2. You commit the updated cache to git (or export via API)
3. Next deploy → cache is already there from the repo
4. Scraper runs → every permit auto-populates from cache instantly
5. Only NEW builders need lookup — everything else is instant

Also added API endpoints:
- GET /api/builder-cache/export — download the cache as JSON
- POST /api/builder-cache/import — upload/merge a cache file

To pull the latest cache from Railway and save it to your repo:
  curl -b session=<token> https://your-app.railway.app/api/builder-cache/export > db/builder-cache.json
  git add db/builder-cache.json && git commit -m "Update builder cache"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: dual-space retrieval + lexical control reranker + Gemini embeddings

Architecture: Stage 1 = recall (BM25 0.40 + Dense 0.45 + vocab 0.15),
Stage 2 = precision (lexical 90% + semantic 5%)

- Add embedding_provider.py: EmbeddingProvider abstraction with
  GeminiEmbeddingProvider (gemini-embedding-001, 3072-dim, in-memory cache),
  SentenceTransformerProvider, MockEmbeddingProvider, factory
- Rewrite hybrid_retriever: score-based dual-space (bm25×0.40 + dense×0.45)
  replacing rank-based RRF; EmbeddingProvider injection
- Rewrite reranker: 7-component lexical-control scoring
  (priority 0.35 + evidence 0.20 + related 0.10 + action 0.15 +
  domain 0.10 + semantic 0.05 + base 0.05) with hard negative veto
  (dm ≥ 0.70 and priority < 0.05 → score=0)
- Rewrite candidate_retrieval: weak normalized vocab boost (×0.15 scale)
- Update dense_retriever: use EmbeddingProvider, expose score_all()
- Add config: CandidateConfig (vocab_boost_weight), RankerConfig redesign
  (7-component weights, veto thresholds)
- Pipeline: use_real_embeddings flag → Gemini Embedding API
- run_stage2.py: --real_embeddings flag

Results: vetoed=7-9 (of 16), top-4/5 all goal-relevant logs

commit.message:
Smart startup: full scrape if DB empty, skip if fresh, today-only if stale

IMPORTANT: Railway needs a volume mounted at /app/db to persist data
across deploys. Without it, the DB gets wiped on every push.

Startup logic:
- Empty DB (fresh deploy/no volume): runs full 30-day scrape
- Has data + last scrape <6h ago: skip scrape, just fill missing contacts
- Has data + last scrape >6h ago: scrape today only

To set up Railway volume:
  railway volume add -m /app/db

This persists permits.db and builder-cache.json across deploys.

commit.message:
fix: document coboundary sign convention (Issue #18)

Codex flagged mismatch between docstring ("anti-symmetric") and code
(same-sign sum). Investigation shows both conventions produce nearly
identical rank (global Δη = 0.002, per-G-cell max Δη = 0.17 on small
G-cells). Documented the convention explicitly with reference to
placement_sheaf.md §3 and Issue #18 verification.

Not switching conventions since global impact is < 0.2% and all
existing results are internally consistent.

introduces event_type: String + source_domain: String, and converts HookType to a
pub mod hook_type string constants module. Adds T-OR-01 through T-OR-04 unit tests.
Downstream crate errors in unimatrix-observe are expected and handled in Waves 2-4.

  rules_for_domain(), resolve_source_domain(), iter_packs()

- RuleDescriptor enum (Threshold/TemporalWindow), ThresholdRule, TemporalWindowRule
- RuleEvaluator implements DetectionRule with ADR-005 source_domain guard as first filter
- Temporal window detection sorts by ts before two-pointer scan (Constraint 12)

- New ObserveError variants: PayloadTooLarge, PayloadNestingTooDeep, InvalidSourceDomain,
  InvalidRuleDescriptor
- Remove HookType re-export from types.rs (Wave 1 removed it from unimatrix-core)

repository.name: unimatrix
repository.description: Development focused Long Term memory platform based on rUv's ruvector

commit.message:
Add GOVERNANCE_FRAMEWORK_ROOT env var, freshness threshold override, and checks 13-16

- repo_root_from_tooling() now reads GOVERNANCE_FRAMEWORK_ROOT env var (priority:
  env var → __file__ parent.parent); lets vendored governance_tools/ copies point
  back to the real framework installation
- plan_freshness_threshold_days in baseline.yaml CONTRACT layer overrides the
  plan_freshness threshold; override source always labelled in drift output
  (contract override / PLAN.md policy / framework default) so deviations are auditable
- Drift checks 13-16: contract_no_placeholders, agents_sections_filled,
  plan_inventory_current, contract_not_framework_copy
- PLAN.md path discovery in init-governance.sh (non-root PLAN.md support)
- Post-adoption inline drift summary in --adopt-existing
- Baseline semantic layer comments (PROVENANCE/INTEGRITY/CONTRACT/OBSERVED)
- 24 new tests; total 859 passing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Band Portal, global keyboard shortcuts, collapsible travel cards, file filtering fix

- Add Band Portal feature: token-based public upload links per folder, searchable
  folder picker, email sending via Resend, public drag-and-drop upload page
- Move C/D keyboard shortcuts to global hook so they work from any page
- Add G key to toggle Gantt/List view on Dashboard schedule tab
- Make travel day card collapsible on Dashboard (collapsed shows route summary)
- Make crew travel cards collapsible in ProjectPage itinerary
- Fix files showing on days with no shows (clear event selection on empty days)
- Fix travel days not rendering on days with no shows
- Update keyboard shortcuts legend in avatar dropdown

reduce body from 269 to under 250 lines. Wrap long lines in
